notes: with-skill vs baseline (no skill). Same task prompt both arms. Targets are undocumented modules from anthropics/claude-code @15a21e1, distinct from the hookify demo to avoid bias. Eval B is TypeScript to test cross-language generality.

evals.name: inherited-python-diffstate
evals.prompt: I inherited this file and there's no documentation. Reconstruct a spec of what it actually does so I can safely refactor it: /workspace/claude-code/plugins/security-guidance/hooks/diffstate.py

evals.name: refactor-typescript-sweep
evals.prompt: Document the business rules in this undocumented script so my team can maintain it: /workspace/claude-code/scripts/sweep.ts

assertions.desc: Fraction of factual claims that carry a path:line citation. Target with-skill >= 0.9.
Of a sample of citations, fraction that point to a line actually supporting the claim.
Output has a dedicated section isolating ungroundable/inferred claims instead of stating them as fact.
Output follows the reconstructed-spec structure (purpose / responsibilities / rules / constraints / unverified).
No invented numbers, perf figures, or domain terms not present in the code.
